channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: NASA's Artemis II Prepares for Historic Moon Mission with Four Astronauts
item.description: NASA is preparing to launch Artemis II, a landmark mission that will send four astronauts on a journey around the moon, marking the first crewed lunar flight in over 50 years. Liftoff is scheduled for...

item.title: Scott Mills addresses past police investigation following dismissal from BBC Radio 2
item.description: Scott Mills, aged 52, has issued a public statement after being dismissed by the BBC over allegations concerning his personal conduct. The announcement follows confirmation that the Metropolitan Polic...

item.title: Phishing scam targets Canadians using closed bread settlement as lure
item.description: Canadian authorities are warning the public about a new phishing scam related to the $500 million bread price-fixing settlement. Scammers are sending fraudulent text messages requesting personal infor...

item.title: Highlights of Notable Australian Book Releases in April 2026
item.description: April sees an eclectic mix of Australian books hitting shelves, spanning fiction, nonfiction, short stories, and cookbooks. Michael Winkler’s 'Griefdogg' explores existential themes through a Mildura ...

item.title: IFC discusses expanding investment, local currency financing and private‑sector growth with Finance Minister Aurangzeb
item.description: A delegation from the International Finance Corporation (IFC), the private‑sector arm of the World Bank Group, met Pakistan’s Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb to discuss its expanding portfolio in ...

item.title: Trump Administration Resumes Worksite Immigration Enforcement Operations
item.description: According to Politico, the Trump administration has reinstated large-scale immigration raids targeting undocumented workers at American worksites, echoing one of the most controversial enforcement tac...

item.title: Veterans Group VoteVets Urges Restraint Amid Rising U.S.-Iran Tensions
item.description: The progressive veterans’ organization VoteVets released a new political advertisement cautioning American leaders against entering another war with Iran. The ad comes as tensions between Washington a...

item.title: Health Secretary Herbosa Faces Graft Complaint Over P1.5 Billion in Wasted Medicines
item.description: The Department of Health (DOH) is under scrutiny after a group of employees identified as 'concerned personnel' filed a graft complaint against Health Secretary Teodoro Herbosa and 16 other officials....

item.title: Woman faces charges following death of elderly woman after assault at Birmingham station
item.description: A woman has been formally charged following the death of Dorothy Chiles, an 87-year-old great-grandmother, after she was assaulted at Birmingham New Street railway station. The incident occurred aroun...

item.title: Teenage girls arrested after Clapham disorder linked to viral online trends
item.description: Six teenage girls have been arrested following two separate outbreaks of anti-social behaviour in south-west London, thought to be driven by viral online trends. Around one hundred police officers wer...

item.title: Analyzing the Economic Impact of War on the U.S.
item.description: This article examines claims regarding the profitability of a current U.S.-involved war. While former President Trump suggested that the country benefits financially due to rising hydrocarbon prices, ...

item.title: US Supreme Court questions legality of Trump’s birthright citizenship directive
item.description: In an unprecedented move, President Donald Trump attended the US Supreme Court on Wednesday as the justices examined his executive order aimed at restricting birthright citizenship. The order directed...

item.title: Businessman Blord Remanded Over Alleged Identity Misuse of Activist VeryDarkMan
item.description: Nigerian cryptocurrency businessman, Linus Williams Ifejirika, known as Blord, don enter serious trouble as Federal High Court Abuja don order say make e dey Kuje Correctional Centre for 26 days. This...

item.title: Dear Killer Nannies: Exploring Trauma and Childhood Amidst Escobar's Shadow
item.description: The Spanish-language series Dear Killer Nannies, co-created by Juan Pablo Escobar, offers a fresh perspective on the life of Pablo Escobar, focusing less on his notorious violence and more on the psyc...

item.title: US Considers Ceasefire with Iran with Pakistan’s Help, Trump Signals Impatience

item.title: Analysis of US-Iran Tensions and Strategic Uncertainty Under Trump
item.description: The article provides an in-depth analysis of escalating tensions between the United States and Iran in early 2026. It highlights the chaotic and contradictory approach of then-President Donald Trump, ...

item.title: Pondicherry University reschedules exams and hostel closures due to LPG supply crisis
item.description: Pondicherry University has revised its academic calendar for the 2025–26 session following an acute shortage of LPG across the region. In a circular, Registrar R. Gunasekaran announced that semester e...

item.title: Araku coffee farmers adopt sustainable practices to combat climate impact
item.description: In Andhra Pradesh's Araku Valley, coffee farmers like Vanthala Raju are facing the challenges of climate change, including erratic rainfall, heat stress, and pest outbreaks. Arabica coffee, sensitive ...

item.title: Albanese’s national address met with mixed reactions as he prepares for Press Club speech
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese delivered a measured address to the nation last night, urging Australians to maintain their daily routines despite the looming challenges posed by the conflict in the M...

item.title: Jain monk claims Maratha queen Tarabai was Jain, stirring backlash from historians and politicians
item.description: A Jain monk, Acharya Nayanpadmasagar, has caused a major controversy in Maharashtra after claiming that Maratha queen Tarabai was a 'Jain queen'. Tarabai, the daughter‑in‑law of Chhatrapati Shivaji Ma...
